reviews.review_text:
Writing this review breaks my heart. I loved Hello Kitty Island Adventure when it first came out, however, ever since Sunblink introduced its monetization statregy I cannot recommend the game.

I'm sure you've read a bunch of other reviews stating that Apple Arcade players receive the content for free that PC players have to pay for. Maybe it was wrong of me and many other players to expect updates and not $25 DLC that Apple Arcade users get for free. Honestly speaking, I feel like this isn't just a problem with Hello Kitty Island Adventure but how the games industry treats girl-centric-gaming in general. Look at similar games that are marketed towards girls. Many of them have egregious prices and egregious dlc on top of the egregiously priced based game. Looking at you, Sims and Disney Dreamlight Valley. 

As much fun as I had playing the game and being hopeful that PC users would receive the same updates Apple Arcade received for free, that's not the case and while I'm disappointed, I can put a game down and stop playing. I cannot support these practices and enabling the studio to do so will signal to them that they can keep taking advantage of us and $25 with barebones content worth of DLC is completely fine.

This game turned out to be a pleasant surprise, it's actually really fun and super cute! I was hesitant to get it, kind of pricey and I didn't know for sure if it was going to be more for little kids but I really wanted to see the Kuromi Halloween island so I took a chance. I'm obsessed with the whole game! I love it! Growing up with all these characters they are fun to see again. So glad I bought it because it really puts a smile on my face and some happy in my heart : )

I love this game so much! I first started playing on my Switch OLED, and I have over 200+ hours on it. There is so much to do in the game, and I really enjoy the slow pace. It gives me something to look forward to and a real sense of achievement while playing. From decorating, crafting cute furniture, solving puzzles, and even the intense but well-worth-it flower system, I can’t get enough of any of it!

If you’re considering this game, please get it — but only if you enjoy slow-paced, achievement-driven, grindy types of games!

40 dollars (80+ if you include DLC) is an absurd price for mobile game. The game is very cute, characters look great, and decorating is fun, but story quests being tied to friendship level makes the game a little tedious, in my opinion. Story quests are unlocked by reaching a specific friendship level, to raise friendship you have to give people gifts and complete daily quests. This means that some days the only things I can do are give everyone 3 coconuts/strawberries/whatever, and do some daily quests. This gameplay loop is fine for Apple Arcade (which I think is 7 dollars a month), but for 40 dollars (80+ with DLC) I can't recommend this, unless you REALLY love Hello Kitty.

This is a great game for my daughter and I've been playing it too to keep up.

Gotta be a girl dad for this one

game is peak, i purchase every dlc the second it comes out and im ever excited to see which sanrio characters get added, also good if you like to 100% catalogs and such in games or quests, I also love the decorating visitor cabins and collecting all the friends

Sunblink Studios has been on a decline since Wheatflour Wonderland. They keep trying to add new content to the game instead of continuing the main gameplay; the Island Mystery Quest (main storyline) has been on hold for nearly a year by now, and there is NOTHING to do in the Wheatflour Wonderland, City Town, Snow Village, or The Moon. Huge map with lots of empty space that you never visit, and characters have limited interaction.

Most people who still play are only completionists or flower collectors.

I started playing Hello Kitty Island Adventure expecting it to just be cute, but it actually ended up being way more fun than I thought. The game has a really relaxing vibe and there’s always something to do without it feeling stressful. Exploring the island, completing little quests, collecting items, and building friendships with all the characters somehow gets really addictive. What surprised me most is how much personality the game has. Every area feels different and the characters actually feel charming instead of just there for decoration. The customization is also really good, especially if you like decorating or making your character look cute. It’s definitely one of those games you play to unwind. The soundtrack, colors, and overall atmosphere are super comforting, and it honestly puts me in a better mood whenever I play it. If you like cozy games, Sanrio characters, or games where you can just relax and explore at your own pace, I’d absolutely recommend it.
Been playing since the day it came out and thoroughly enjoy it, The daily gifts thing sometimes is a pain, but it's still fun

My husband has come home almost everyday since I’ve gotten this game to me rotting on the couch playing. It is so cozy and cute without being one of those typical farm sim type games. The paid content is a little lackluster but I’m hoping it will improve with time. I love exploring all the different areas and befriending all the characters as I go. If you are a completionist you will have many, many, many hours of work to do.
One of the most fun and addicting games of all time. Butters would be proud.
This game is really cozy & cute! If you want an animal crossing experience on pc with the charm of sanrio characters, this is as close as it gets!
ah, yes. my favorite game that I spend all my time with my Computer on; Hello Kitty Island Adventure

It took over twenty-X years for me to finally get into this game. Ever since the South Park Episode "Make Love, Not Warcraft" back in 2006, I been joking around to play Hello Kitty Island Adventure. It is totally worth it.

They lied to us about microtransactions. 
I was really excited about HKIA when it released since they told us that there will be no microtransactions in the future, but they completely walked back on that statement a month ago. AND they’re choosing to double down instead of consider that their microtransactions are a terrible idea. 
They keep repeating the gambling part, we’re all very aware they’re not loot boxes but they’re still microtransactions, no quotes needed on their part. 
They can try to call it what they want but treating their community like they can’t understand basic concepts is disgusting. What an absolute disrespect to their community to be treated in such way, and while they’re trying to milk their community for every last cent while they’re doing it too. Disappointing. 
A lot of us have already spent $50+ on this game LET ALONE those of us that got the Deluxe Edition (me included) that thought we were supporting a good dev team that wasn't going to gaslight and lie to it's own community. Shameful. Don't waste a cent on this game.

for some reason this game is actually very fun, i mean if your into this types of games you may enjoy it alot more unsuprisingly but i play lots of survival horror, shooters and action/rpg games, so this is an odd one. Its actually pretty fun. You can so easily get distracted doing something else, for example oh im gonna do this quest then immediatly see a collectible and go for it and keep exploring its very hard to actually stay focused on what your doing, or atleast for me personally. But i actually like that, there is so much area to explore, so many collectibles that you see and when you see another one really close and then there is gifting stuff to villagers, baking, crafting, fishing, house decorating collectible searching. there is so much stuff to do there is also lots of area to explore. My biggest problem so far though is the fetch questy type quests. Also this game was pretty expensive ($36 aud on sale) but yes i very much recommend this game although dont think im far enough in to really recommend it well, but yes i recommend and its slightly amusing seeing resident evil 4 and hello kitty next to each other

It's a cute, fun, and simple game that you can kind of turn your brain off for. I dont love that the extra characters only come on certain in real time days and you have to keep up with that if you want them to be added to the island. Feels too much like a gimmick to get you to play more often and I feel it's too much of pawn. Otherwise, super fun and chill.

I don't play world of warcraft...
Yeah but I'm playin hello kitty island adventure!
Thank you butters for putting me on this game 10/10 so cute and totally adorable!! It's such a fun and cozy game to play that'll just make you feel relaxed :3

Did not think I would get as obsessed with this game as I did, but here we are, two save files and 254 hours later... I think this might be my highest (tracked) hour count in a non-idle game? I'd have to double check that.
The fact that this game has successfully convinced me to check into it every day for multiple months is crazy impressive. Surprisingly enjoyable story? Did not come into this expecting one but I really liked it, very sweet.

Also, task succeeded failfully. Unfortunately the character this game most made me want merch of is the one character who is made up just for this game and thus has zero official merch.

I am not the target demographic for this game, I know very little about Hello Kitty and yet I find this game a joy to play and mindlessly compelling. Its an easy recommend for EVERYONE on planet earth cause its good enough to have absolute universal appeal.
a good game to unwind & be stress free & it's super cute, glad i got it on sale!

I love this game, it's adorable and great for everyone who loves Hello Kitty. However the achievements are a PAIN to get, specifically one of the visitor achievements which is obtaining Milk on your island. I've played since the game launched on steam, and have only recently got Milk on my island and gotten the achievement. That was a year and a half of working towards this one achievement. It shouldn't take that long for one single achievement. Other than that, the game is really relaxing, the devs are on top of bug fixes and communicating with the community, and have a steady flow of both paid and free updates coming out.
Peak game. Very easy to understand and very fun to play. 100/10

This is a really fun and cute game, but I would recommend waiting for a sale because the MSRP is a bit steep for a mobile game port. I love the character customization and the colourful graphics. Music is a bit generic, but this is definitely the kind of game where I have my own music or TV on anyway. If you're a Sanrio fan who enjoys chill Animal Crossing-style gameplay then you'll have a good time!
Very cozy, very addictive. 9/10 would recommend, and the 1 just doesn't prefer the fixed camera angle.
Cute and relaxing game. and cozy to play with a friend but multiplayer is a bit lacking content for player 2.
This game is so fun. Didn't know any thing about Hello Kitty or Sanrio before this. It's been nice meeting the characters and learning who everyone is. I'm having a blast.
HIghly recommend playing this game with a controller on the PC. Makes it so much easier. Very addictive, lots to collect, quests to do and places to explore.
I love the game, amazing graphics and story line

Nobody understands me. They don’t know why I’ve put over 100 hours into Hello Kitty Island Adventure. If I were to sum it up, first, this is “Happy: The Video Game.” I was never stressed or frustrated. It’s just good vibes all around.

And second, it’s also “Things: The Video Game.” Thanks to this game’s mobile, pseudo-live service origins, you get to play through this game’s insane update history, basically in order of its release. So it’s secretly a super game. A super game full of stuff, and things. 

There’s always some kind of goal: there’s a new quest everyday, some kind of thing to check off your list, some new item to collect, some new visitor to bring to your island. You never need to go searching for something to do. I just unlocked a new area… at the 113 hour mark. And come to think of it, there is so much dialogue in this game, the script would probably rival that of full-blown JRPGs, I’m not kidding.

I do think I’ve had my fill though. If I had just two complaints, the last achievement is kind of annoying (I’m just waiting until the game wants me to play it again), and the price of the second DLC is pretty close to that of the game itself (and even more when it’s on sale), so I’d need to see a steep discount before I even consider it. I wouldn’t let that get in the way of playing this game if you’re interested though. Obviously, I didn’t need it.

Other than that, probably the greatest video game ever made, probably.
very cozy and laid-back!! lots to do, lots of things you can unlock, easy and low stress gameplay, very cute!!!

I love Hello Kitty everything. While I understand that creating the game with a maximum daily quest availability so one does not play for 16 hours strait, I don't like seeing all the locked quests in my que because I can only give three gifts a day per character to build friendship level to do those quests. On the bright side, it is colorful and cute and overall I like the gameplay.
The game is addictive. The only thing I dislike is that you have to wait to the next human day for almost all quests.

Cute cozy game, once you play a bit more you’ll have a lot to do. Characters are so cute. Fun monthly events and cute outfits and furniture you can get. For certain things it can get boring at times but just gotta find things to do, and collect collect collect!!

I am a HUGE fan of this game. It's super fun and always changing, so I find i rarely get bored with it. The endless amount of content makes me want to keep coming back to it. It also works well with either controller or keyboard.

"You may not like it but this is what peak gaming looks like" -Butters, probably

Bought it as a half-gag because I love Sanrio but this game is genuinely great. I was baffled by the amount of content and lore this game has offered, how flashed out and palpable all the characters feel, not to mention the laughs and emotional moments I got out of its story. It was a clear passion project of a lot of devs who love Sanrio at Sunblink. Also, it's truly for all ages - it's simple enough for kids, very family friendly and positive (without being pandering/fake) but A LOT of adult references and self-awareness are sneaked in here and there.

Drawbacks are that it was clearly developed with mobile/console in mind, you can't upgrade the cabin size (which sucks if you really wanna decorate) and also your character not having name/bday is a major oversight for this kind of game. But overall great game, I genuinely believe it made me a more positive person. I wish all my friends would play it. One of the greatest newgen Sanrio pieces of media - 8.5/10

Hello Kitty Island Adventure is soooo cute! And while it's easy enough for my 5 year old to play, it's also interesting enough and funny enough for me to enjoy too. I absolutely love this game. It has become one of my top 3 favorites :) It works great on the Legion Go and my laptop

My new video game obsession!  If you love Hello Kitty, this game is for you.  If you love cozy games, this game is for you.  Easy to learn, easy to play.  Beautiful graphics.  So far, the only downfall for me is not being able to pan around my avatar to see my surroundings.
O jogo tem sido uma experiência muito boa, tem uma grande exploração e é muito fofo.

I love this comfy game. You can really tell from all the little details that the developers have put a lot of care into it. It’s well made, I haven’t come across any bugs, the visuals are spot on for the Sanrio brand, and I love that they release regular updates, daily and weekly missions, and new DLCs. Highly recommended.

i think this game single handily cured my depression. i love it so much! <333
i have almost 200 hours of game time and i still haven't come close to completing all of the quests. i love the dlc's as well. so so so cutie pie and fun !! >.<
one of my most favorite games ever. if you're a sanrio fanatic, i'd absolutely recommend you play it !!!

I got hyped up about this game by watching a streamer play it, but it turns out that it's a walking simulator that's also very grindy. I read other reviews and saw that the expensions were disappointing the players and that the studio was also starting to use AI apparently ? Yikes. After 84 mins, I'm glad I made the choice to get a refund because that was not doing it for me. I was bored out of my mind.

It's a lot like animal crossing but with a plot line and no consequences for ignoring your villagers for several days. The characters are super cute and the gameplay is fun. plenty of places to explore and quests to do. it makes for a relaxing time sink if you want to do a bunch of things. Items respawn on the island daily. daily quests and story ones so you can spend as much or as little time as you want in game.

My 5 year old loves this game, interactive with the characters, knowing their names. Even though she cannot read yeat she is able to navigate with minimum help and always find something to do, with a little help navigate trough the quests. Controller support is good, no issues with the game whatsoever. The game is overall very nicely done, graphics, music, game mechanics.
Super cute and mindless gameplay.

What can I say? It's Hello Kitty Island Adventure, I guess.

But let me break it down for you, Mark.
This game is PHENOMENAL. Everything, every single part of it is pure perfection.
The writing? Better than Baldurs Gate 3.
The characters? Better designs than any video game ever made.
The world? SUPERIOR TO EVERYTHING YOU'VE EVER SEEN.

The game boasts 1000+ hours of content, a huge world to explore on land and underwater, amazing, rich of content sidequests that do not ever get repetetive, and the main story.. wow.. It's just amazing..

I could only recommend this game.
No. This piece of art.

Try it for yourself, and be forever amazed by Hello Kitty Island Adventure.
GREATES GAME OF ALL TIME I LOVE HELLO KITTY DID I LAOS MENTION I LISTEN TOLAUFEY AND HAVE A TOTE BAG AND DRINK MATCHA AND BOBA CHAI TEA

This is really fun game I love Lego games because like this its open world u can go anywhere do anything at any time! I LOVE IT!!!! 💜💜💜

One of my favorite games ever. Such a fun time to just play for a few hours a day. The puzzles aren't too challenging and the events are super fun. I keep thinking the game is about to end and then I unlock a new area. It got me super into Hello Kitty characters, too! I quintessential cozy game in my opinion. The plot is really good, too. They all have a lot of personality.
